phases.result:
Planning pipeline complete. One Story persisted and set to `agent::ready`.

## Summary

**Story #5 — "Expose customer endpoints under /clients with /customers as a deprecated alias"**
🔗 https://github.com/dsj1984/.../issues/5 · label `plan-run::02cdedc7` · `agent::ready`

**Why one Story:** The two halves (new `/clients` surface + `/customers` deprecated alias) are tightly coupled — they share the same service, repository, and data. Splitting would violate the default-single policy's "near-zero overlap / architectural seam" test.

**The contract folded into `## Spec`:**
- All five ops (`POST`, `GET` list, `GET /:id`, `PATCH`, `DELETE`) served under `/clients` with byte-identical behaviour to today's `/customers` routes — same status codes, bodies, auth, `requireRole('admin')` on delete, validation, and `cus_` id prefix.
- **Same data through both prefixes** — backed by the unchanged `customers.service.js`/repo, so a record created via one path is visible via the other (explicit acceptance criterion + no data-layer fork).
- `/customers` kept as a working **deprecated alias**, flagged via a `Deprecation` response header without changing the body; `/clients` carries no such header.
- Preserve conventions: error envelope via `sendError`/`ApiError`, `routes→services→repositories` layering, and share handler logic rather than copy-paste.

**Verification:** new `tests/clients.test.js` (contract), unchanged `tests/customers.test.js` must still pass (proves the alias), `npm test`, `npm run lint`.

**Gates:** interrogate (no duplicates) → author → critics (consolidation + pre-mortem both skipped/proceed; one advisory text-hygiene citation fixed) → dry-run persist (all validators green) → persist. HITL gates #1/#2 auto-proceeded under `--yes`. No delivery run, per instructions.

Deliver later with `/deliver 5`.

**Story #5 delivered and landed.** Here is the terminal envelope:

```json
{"kind":"story-deliver-terminal","storyId":5,"status":"landed","phase":"post-land",
 "storyBranch":"story-5","baseBranch":"main",
 "pr":{"number":6,"url":".../pull/6","autoMergeEnabled":true,"state":"MERGED"},
 "gates":{"validation":"passed","baseSync":"passed","codeReview":"passed"},
 "tail":{"followUps":true,"statusResync":true,"refCleanup":true,"baseFastForward":false},
 "failure":null,"nextCommand":null,"elapsedSeconds":17}
```

## Summary

The planning pipeline had produced a **Story-only** ticket (v2 model — no `type::epic`): **Story #5, "Expose customer endpoints under /clients with /customers as a deprecated alias."** I delivered the existing plan unchanged.

**What shipped** (PR #6, squash-merged to `main`, Story `agent::done`):
- `registerCustomerRoutes` parameterized over a base path — `/clients` (canonical) and `/customers` (deprecated alias) register from **one shared definition**, backed by the same service, repository, and rows. No data-layer fork, no duplicated handlers.
- A `markDeprecated` prelude sets `Deprecation: true` on **all** `/customers` responses (success and error paths, since `res.setHeader` survives `writeHead`); `/clients` responses carry no such header. Response bodies, status codes, auth/role, validation, and the error envelope are byte-identical across both prefixes.
- `tests/clients.test.js` covering all five ACs (CRUD+list parity, cross-prefix data visibility both directions, the deprecation-header contrast with identical bodies, admin-only delete). `tests/helpers/http.js` extended to expose response headers (backward-compatible).

**Gates:** full suite 137/137 pass (existing `tests/customers.test.js` unmodified vs `main` and all passing — AC-3); lint/format clean; fresh maker-blind acceptance-critic scored all 5 criteria `met` → gate `proceed`; Story-scope code review returned 0 findings.

One benign degradation: the post-land fast-forward of local `main` was skipped because the main checkout had pre-existing uncommitted changes (the `package.json` / `plan-metrics.json` dirt present before this session) — the merge stands regardless. Single-Story run, so the per-run epilogue is skipped.
